epiwk.long_description: The epidemiological year runs from the start of July to the end of June the following calendar year. For example the 2024 epidemiological year starts first week of July 2024 and ends the last week of June 2025. The epidemiological week indexes the epi year, with week 1 in July, and week 52 in the following June

nrevss.name: National Respiratory and Enteric Virus Surveillance System (NREVSS)
nrevss.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
nrevss.description: The National Respiratory and Enteric Virus Surveillance System (NREVSS) is a voluntary, laboratory-based surveillance system that monitors temporal and geographic trends for respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), human parainfluenza viruses, respiratory adenoviruses, human metapneumovirus, human coronaviruses, and rotavirus circulation in the United States. Participating laboratories report weekly to CDC on the number of tests performed and the number positive for each virus. NREVSS data are used to characterize seasonal patterns of these viruses and to help public health officials anticipate and prepare for outbreaks. Data are aggregated at the HHS regional and national levels. The system has been operational since 1987 and includes approximately 300 participating laboratories across the United States.
